title: Claude Design Tutorial: Build a Website in Minutes with AI
summary: Claude Design is an Anthropic feature that generates website prototypes, landing pages, and presentations from text prompts. Users can create designs using pre-built templates or custom specifications, then export functional code for web pages.
writeup: Claude Design, a new Anthropic feature inside claude.ai, lets users generate functional HTML/CSS prototypes, slide decks, and landing pages through a single chat prompt. The video walks through redesigning a personal AI tutorial site by feeding Claude the site URL, channel descriptions, and a structured brief, with Claude autonomously screenshotting the source, web-searching for context, and generating all assets in roughly five minutes. The result includes a dark/light theme, working outbound links, categorized video cards, and downloadable project files, with a section-level feedback loop for iterative refinement.
key_points: Claude Design is accessed via the claude.ai sidebar and offers four starting modes: prototype (app UI, website layouts, dashboards), slide deck, from template, and a flexible other category.

A single detailed prompt that included the site URL, YouTube channel descriptions, content categories (AI, data science, generative AI, agentic AI), a GitHub code link, and social profile links was sufficient to produce a complete, themed landing page in approximately five minutes.

Claude autonomously took a screenshot of the existing site, performed a web search about it, and fetched its data before generating HTML, CSS, button, font, badge, and link files, showing the agent-driven scope of the tool beyond simple code generation.

The generated design included a functioning dark/light theme toggle, clickable category sections with structured video cards, and all external links wired to GitHub, Instagram, LinkedIn, and the YouTube channels.
Each generated section includes inline feedback controls, a looks good button and a need work button, allowing Claude to revise individual sections without regenerating the whole page.
Completed designs are exported by clicking Share then Download project as ZIP, yielding a self-contained folder with an index.html that opens directly in a browser.
